schema_json.description:
LLM contract for the agentic search loop 'Reason' step.

After each round of searches, the LLM analyzes results and decides
whether to continue searching or converge. Generates targeted
follow-up queries informed by what was found so far.

key_findings.description: 3-5 key findings from the latest round
key_findings.title: Key Findings

perspective_gaps.description: Underrepresented STORM perspectives in current results
perspective_gaps.title: Perspective Gaps

web_queries.description: 3-6 web queries for the next round
web_queries.title: Web Queries

academic_queries.description: 1-3 Semantic Scholar queries using precise terminology
academic_queries.title: Academic Queries

exa_queries.description: 0-1 semantic queries for Exa neural search
exa_queries.title: Exa Queries

should_continue.description: Whether another search round is needed
should_continue.title: Should Continue

reasoning.description: Brief rationale for the convergence assessment
reasoning.title: Reasoning

knowledge_gaps.description: Specific knowledge gaps identified from reading content
knowledge_gaps.title: Knowledge Gaps

schema_json.title: AgenticRoundAnalysis

prompt:
You are a research search strategist conducting an agentic deep research loop.

RESEARCH QUESTION: What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting in adults with metabolic syndrome?

ROUND HISTORY:
Round 1: 9 queries -> 79 web + 40 academic, 113 new URLs, 6 pages read

  Summary: Abstract Background Previous studies have demonstrated that intermittent fasting (IF) has garnered scientific attention and gained recognition for its beneficial effects on metabolic outcomes. Accordingly, this systematic review and meta-analysis aimed to evaluate the effect of fasting on glycemic control, lipid profile, and inflammatory markers. Methods Databases such as PubMed, Embase, Cochrane, Scopus, and Web of Science were used to retrieve relevant studies published until September 2025.

[Page 6] The Role of Intermittent Fasting on Metabolic Syndrome
  Summary: Abstract Intermittent fasting has gained popularity as a dietary intervention to improve metabolic health. Metabolic syndrome may benefit from intermittent fasting by improving weight, cholesterol levels, blood pressure (BP), and glucose control. This study aims to assess the effects of intermittent fasting on weight, BMI, cholesterol levels, BP, and glucose in individuals with metabolic syndrome.
  Perspectives: Scientific
  Key facts: Analysis showed significant improvements in weight, with a mean reduction of 3.59 kg (95% CI: -4.59 to -2.59, p < 0.0001) and a decrease in BMI of 1.39 kg/m2 (95% CI: -1.87 to -0.92, p < 0.0001).; Low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol levels dropped by 56.22 mg/dL (95% CI: -80.14 to -32.29, p < 0.0001), and systolic BP decreased by 5.54 mmHg (95% CI: -7.55 to -3.53, p < 0.0001).; These risk factors include elevated blood pressure (BP), high blood sugar, excess body fat around the waist, and abnormal cholesterol or triglyceride levels [2].

PERSPECTIVE COVERAGE:
  Covered (5/9): Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health
  Uncovered: Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends
  Low coverage: Regulatory, Industry, Public_Health

Based on what has been READ:
1. Decompose the research question into 5-8 sub-questions.
   Which sub-questions have ZERO or WEAK evidence?
   Which sub-questions are well-answered with strong evidence?
2. What CONTRADICTIONS exist between sources? Flag conflicting evidence.
3. What specific KNOWLEDGE GAPS remain? List them as specific factual questions
   we CANNOT yet answer. 'none' is almost never correct for a complex research question.
4. Rate overall confidence in accumulated knowledge: LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH with reasoning.
5. Which perspectives need more coverage?
6. Generate 3-6 targeted WEB queries to fill those gaps.
7. Generate 1-3 ACADEMIC queries using precise terminology.
8. Optionally generate 0-1 semantic EXA queries.
9. Assess ANSWER COMPLETENESS for the research question:
   - List specific sub-questions that remain UNANSWERED.
   - For each answered sub-question, rate evidence strength (weak/moderate/strong).
   - 'expanding': Major sub-questions still unanswered.
   - 'narrowing': Most sub-questions answered, seeking confirmation/depth.
   - 'saturated': All sub-questions answered with strong evidence.
10. Should we continue searching?

Focus on queries that address content-level gaps, not just missing URLs.
